maxContractTerm.value: 5 years (with optional annual renewals on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local school board after the initial term)
maxContractTerm.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(D): 'A school district may enter into a school bus service contract for a term not to exceed five years. A school bus service contract may provide, at the expiration of the term of the contract, for annual renewal of the school bus service contract on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local school board.' Implementing rule: 6.43.2.8(G) NMAC: 'The local board may agree to a five-year contract period. ... At the end of the contract period the contract may be renewed annually on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local board.' Re-verified verbatim against (i) the official Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 / HB 485 session-law PDF on nmlegis.gov and (ii) the NM Public Education Department's official posting of 22-16-3 NMSA 1978 on its School Transportation State Statutes and Regulations page (PED PDF dated August 2024).
maxContractTerm.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified state statute, official PED posting) + Tier 1 (session law, official Legislature PDF) + Tier 2 (administrative code, official Records Center publisher)

rebidRequired.value: Not required - at expiration of the awarded five-year term, the local school board may renew the existing school bus service contract annually on the same terms and conditions without running a new competitive procurement (NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(D); 6.43.2.8(G) and 6.43.2.12 NMAC). A full competitive process under 6.43.2.11 NMAC (RFP, ten-day notice, board-developed evaluation criteria, award to most efficient/economical/safe proposal) is required only when a NEW contract is awarded - e.g., retirement/resignation/death of the current contractor; change from district-owned to contract operation; addition of buses unless the board elects to amend an existing contract under § 22-16-3; unsatisfactory services; or termination of the existing contract. Separately, NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H) categorically EXEMPTS 'contracts with businesses for public school transportation services' from the entire Procurement Code, so the general competitive-bidding requirements of NMSA 1978, § 13-1-102 and the general multi-term cap in § 13-1-150 do not apply to school bus service contracts; the school-specific rule in § 22-16-3 and 6.43.2 NMAC controls.
rebidRequired.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(D) (annual renewal option); NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H) ('The provisions of the Procurement Code shall not apply to: ... H. contracts with businesses for public school transportation services'); 6.43.2.11 NMAC (procedures for awarding NEW contracts - RFP required); 6.43.2.12 NMAC (procedures for renewing existing contracts - financial statement, ownership disclosure, board evaluation of services, notice to state transportation director on PED forms; no rebid). Re-verified verbatim against (i) the official 2012 NM Procurement Code compendium published by the NM Supreme Court, which prints § 13-1-98(H) word-for-word and includes the Morningstar annotation confirming the Code's exemptions apply to school districts, and (ii) the NM Records Center 6.43.2 NMAC posting.
rebidRequired.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified state statute) + Tier 2 (administrative code, official Records Center publisher)

renewalMechanics.value: After the initial five-year term, the local school board may, at its option, renew the school bus service contract annually 'on the same terms and conditions' (NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(D); 6.43.2.8(G) NMAC; PED-approved fleet contract form 6.43.2.13(A) and individual owner-operator contract form 6.43.2.14(A), each providing 'This contract may be renewed annually thereafter on the same terms and conditions at the option of the BOARD'). Any change in contract terms requires renegotiation/amendment (6.43.2.8(G) NMAC). Before each renewal, 6.43.2.12 NMAC requires the district to (a) obtain a current financial statement from the contractor; (b) obtain corporate ownership/officer information; (c) have the local board evaluate the contractor's services against the contract terms and determine whether the contract should be renewed; (d) apprise the contractor of evaluation results - if renewed despite identified deficiencies, the renewal terms must ensure those deficiencies are corrected; (e) disclose any anticipated boundary/route/attendance-center changes; (f) confirm the renewal amount does not exceed available resources; and (g) provide notice of the renewal to the state transportation director on PED-approved forms. By statute and rule the renewal-rate framework must reflect fuel costs, operation and maintenance costs, and (for fleet contractors) employee salary and benefits costs (NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(C); 6.43.2.8(D) NMAC). No statutory CPI/index escalator, fuel escalator, automatic/evergreen rollover, third-party-agency approval, or annual-termination clause is specified for the annual renewal; renewal is at the local school board's option. (The PED-approved forms 6.43.2.13 and 6.43.2.14 contain detailed for-cause termination provisions but no 'annual termination' right.)
renewalMechanics.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(C)-(D); 6.43.2.8(D), (G) NMAC; 6.43.2.12 NMAC; 6.43.2.13(A) NMAC (fleet form, TERM clause); 6.43.2.14(A) NMAC (individual owner-operator form, TERM clause).
renewalMechanics.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified state statute) + Tier 2 (administrative code, official Records Center publisher)

statutoryCitation.value: Primary (transportation-specific): NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 (School Bus Service Contracts) - last amended by Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485, 2009 Regular Session), effective June 19, 2009 (re-verified: no later amendments located in 2010-2026 sessions). Implementing administrative code: 6.43.2 NMAC (Requirements for School Bus Contracts and Per Capita Feeder Agreements), effective 12-31-1998, last amended 11-13-2009; statutory authority recited in 6.43.2.3 NMAC is NMSA 1978, §§ 22-2-1, 22-16-2, 22-16-3, 22-16-6, and 22-8-27. General procurement exemption that explains why the Procurement Code's multi-term and competitive-bidding rules do not control school bus contracts: NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H). (For context only - does not control: NMSA 1978, § 13-1-150 caps general multi-term contracts of $25,000 or more at 10 years (4 years for professional services); § 13-1-98(H)'s categorical exemption removes school bus contracts from the entire Procurement Code, leaving the school-specific 5-year-plus-annual-renewal regime in § 22-16-3 to control.)
statutoryCitation.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3; NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H); 6.43.2 NMAC; Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485, 2009 Regular Session, applicability to contracts and contract renewals entered into on or after June 19, 2009).
statutoryCitation.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified state statute, official PED posting) + Tier 1 (session law) + Tier 2 (administrative code) + Tier 5 (school-boards-association sample policy confirming the framework)

districtVariance.value: Unknown - no Attorney General opinion, State Auditor report, PED bulletin, or other authoritative statewide source was located that documents differing district interpretations of NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 or 6.43.2 NMAC. The framework appears to be applied uniformly statewide because (a) all school bus service contracts must be in writing on forms approved by the Public Education Department (NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(B); 6.43.2.8(C); 6.43.2.13 (fleet contract form); 6.43.2.14 (individual owner-operator form)); (b) the PED reviews proposals/awards and may audit the local board's procurement records (6.43.2.11(H) NMAC); (c) NMSBA Sample Policy EE (Transportation Services) and Sample Policy EEAB (Special Education Student Transportation) state the five-year term and the PED-approved-form requirement without flagging interpretive variation. The challenge round confirmed that two large districts (Albuquerque Public Schools and Las Cruces Public Schools) operate within this framework: APS issues RFPs under § 13-1-21 in-state-preference provisions, and Las Cruces approves school-bus contracts on an annual cadence with STS of New Mexico - both consistent with § 22-16-3's 5-year-plus-annual-renewal regime. However, the absence of an authoritative statewide survey of district-level practice means the variance question cannot be answered definitively from official sources.
districtVariance.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3(B); 6.43.2.8(C), 6.43.2.11(H), 6.43.2.13, 6.43.2.14 NMAC; NMSBA Sample Policy EE (z2.ctspublish.com/nmsba/DocViewer.jsp?doccode=z20000167); NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB (z2.ctspublish.com/nmsba/DocViewer.jsp?doccode=z20000173).

specialEducation.differentRules: No - New Mexico does NOT set separate state-law term limits, rebid requirements, procurement rules, or renewal mechanics for special-education / IEP-mandated / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / disability transportation when the service is delivered through a private school bus contractor.
specialEducation.explanation: The contractual framework in NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 and 6.43.2 NMAC applies to all 'school bus service contracts' between school districts and private contractors regardless of the student population served. NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H) exempts 'contracts with businesses for public school transportation services' from the entire Procurement Code without distinguishing among student categories. The state's special-education transportation regulation framework lives in 6.31.2 NMAC (special education) and 6.41.4 NMAC (general transportation operations and standards for providing transportation for eligible students, including students with disabilities, foster youth, and homeless youth); neither imposes a separate term cap, competitive-procurement requirement, or renewal mechanism for special-ed transportation contracts. 6.41.4 NMAC requires LEAs to ensure 'properly executed contract on a form approved by the department' but specifies no duration restrictions. Federal IDEA (34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.107) and the McKinney-Vento Act govern the duty to provide transportation as a related service (e.g., IEP-required transport, transportation to school of origin) but do not establish state contract or procurement rules; this is reflected in NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB, which references 6.41.4 NMAC and 34 C.F.R. provisions for the substantive special-ed transportation obligations and contains no provisions about contract term limits or procurement procedures. Therefore, special-ed transportation services procured through a private school bus contractor are subject to the same 5-year-term-plus-annual-renewal rule as regular-route services.
specialEducation.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3; NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98(H); 6.43.2 NMAC; 6.41.4 NMAC; 6.31.2 NMAC; NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB (Special Education Student Transportation).

maxContractTerm.note: Re-verified verbatim against TWO independent official sources: (1) the Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485) session-law PDF on nmlegis.gov, and (2) the NM Public Education Department's posting of NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 on its School Transportation State Statutes and Regulations page. Both confirm the exact text of Subsection D quoted by Stage 1. No amendment to § 22-16-3 located in the 2010-2026 sessions. The implementing rule 6.43.2.8(G) NMAC was re-verified verbatim against the NM Records Center / Archives (official NMAC publisher) and matches Stage 1's quote.

rebidRequired.note: Re-verified verbatim: § 13-1-98(H) ('contracts with businesses for public school transportation services') was confirmed word-for-word in the 2012 NM Procurement Code compendium published on the NM Supreme Court website (sole-source PDF page 34). The Morningstar annotation in that compendium confirms § 13-1-98's exemptions reach school district contracts. The 6.43.2.11 NMAC RFP procedure (for new contracts only) and 6.43.2.12 NMAC renewal procedure (no rebid required) re-verified. The Cuddy & McCarthy NMSBA presentation independently confirms: 'Contracts for public school transportation services' is listed among Procurement Code exemptions under § 13-1-98. No exception, sole-source exception, or other authority found that would re-impose a rebid requirement at five-year-term expiration.

renewalMechanics.note: Re-verified verbatim. The PED-approved fleet and individual-owner-operator contract forms (6.43.2.13(A) and 6.43.2.14(A) NMAC) each state, in identical language: 'This contract may be renewed annually thereafter on the same terms and conditions at the option of the BOARD.' The procedural prerequisites in 6.43.2.12 NMAC re-verified verbatim. Challenge search for 'annual termination' / 'CPI escalator' / 'evergreen' / 'automatic renewal' / 'third-party approval' provisions returned nothing in § 22-16-3 or 6.43.2 NMAC beyond the rate-structure recognition of fuel and operation/maintenance costs in § 22-16-3(C) and 6.43.2.8(D). PED-approved forms do contain detailed for-cause termination clauses, but NO annual-termination right - confirming the 'X years plus annual renewal at board option' framing rather than an 'X years with annual termination permitted' framing.

statutoryCitation.note: Re-verified that NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 was last amended by Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485, 2009 Regular Session), effective June 19, 2009, with applicability to contracts including renewals entered into on or after that date. Bill PDF retrieved from nmlegis.gov and OCR-extracted; text matches Stage 1. NMPED Bill Analyses index and the NM Bill Tracker were checked for 2024-2026 session bills affecting § 22-16-3 - none located. 6.43.2 NMAC effective 12-31-1998, last amended 11-13-2009 - confirmed via the NM Records Center / Archives posting. § 13-1-98(H) confirmed via the NM Supreme Court Procurement Code compendium; § 13-1-150 confirmed via the same source (with current law for the $25k+ bracket being 10 years per Cuddy & McCarthy and Justia 2025 compilation, raised from 8 years by a later amendment) - this section remains contextual only because of § 13-1-98(H) exemption.

districtVariance.note: Original verdict 'Unknown' preserved verbatim. Adversarial challenge attempted: searched for differing AG opinions, State Auditor reports, PED bulletins, and large + small district policies. None of the search paths produced credible disconfirming authority - the uniform PED-approved contract-form requirement (6.43.2.13/.14 NMAC) and the absence of any AG opinion interpreting § 22-16-3 strongly suggest uniform application, but no authoritative statewide survey of district-level practice was located. Confirmed Albuquerque Public Schools and Las Cruces Public Schools operate within the § 22-16-3 framework on the limited public information available, but two-district spot-checks do not establish statewide uniformity. The negative-result finding stands; verdict downgraded from 'confirmed' to 'unresolved' to reflect that 'Unknown' itself is a negative result on an unanswerable-from-official-sources question.

specialEducation.note: Re-verified: 6.41.4 NMAC (official PED PDF, current rule) contains NO contract-term-limit, competitive-procurement, or renewal-mechanism provisions for special-education or any other student-population transportation contracts; it regulates only operational standards and driver/bus requirements. NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB (re-fetched directly) contains 'no provisions about contract term limits or procurement procedures' for special-education transportation, confirming reliance on the general § 22-16-3 / 6.43.2 NMAC framework. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento provisions cited are duty-to-provide rules, not state contract/procurement rules. No separate IEP / foster-care / homeless / alternative-student transportation contracting rule located in 6.31.2 NMAC, 6.41.4 NMAC, or 6.43.2 NMAC.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term - 5 years with annual renewal at board option
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A later session law (2010-2026), AG opinion, administrative rule, or special-procurement provision could extend, override, or grandfather a longer term; § 22-16-3 might have been renumbered or repealed; the general procurement multi-term cap in § 13-1-150 (10 years for $25k+ contracts) might still control because § 13-1-98(H) might apply only to a sub-set of school transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched nmlegis.gov 2010-2026 session bills for amendments to 22-16-3; searched for 'notwithstanding'/'successive'/'extension' in the school bus contract context; re-opened § 13-1-98(H) on the NM Supreme Court Procurement Code PDF and verified the categorical exemption; re-opened § 13-1-150 to confirm its non-applicability under the H exemption; cross-checked Justia 2024/2025 § 22-16-3 against the NM PED official posting and Laws 2009 ch. 92 § 2.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding holds. § 22-16-3 has not been amended since 2009. § 13-1-98(H) is a categorical exemption confirmed verbatim against the NM Supreme Court compendium and the Cuddy & McCarthy presentation. § 13-1-150 cannot reach school bus service contracts because of the H exemption. No grandfather, successive-extension, or notwithstanding provision found.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confidence remains High on max contract term.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End - Not required (annual renewal without competitive procurement)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Mandatory bidding language in § 13-1-102, § 22-5-4, or a school-specific provision might re-impose a competitive procurement at renewal; PED rules might require a fresh RFP at the end of a five-year term; the Procurement Code exemption might not extend to renewals.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 13-1-102 (general competitive sealed-bid mandate) - applicability gated by § 13-1-98 exemptions. Re-opened § 13-1-98(H): 'contracts with businesses for public school transportation services' - no carve-back for renewals. Re-opened 6.43.2.11 NMAC (new contracts - RFP required) vs. 6.43.2.12 NMAC (renewals - evaluation only, no rebid). Cuddy & McCarthy presentation listed 'Contracts for public school transportation services' among items 'Exempt from the Procurement Code'.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding holds. The H exemption is total; § 22-16-3(D)'s annual-renewal-at-board-option language and 6.43.2.12 NMAC's evaluation-only procedure govern. No mandatory rebid at term-end is imposed by any locatable authority.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confidence remains High on rebid requirement.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics - annual renewal on same terms and conditions, no escalator/evergreen/third-party approval
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An automatic / evergreen rollover, CPI or fuel escalator, public-hearing requirement, maximum-number-of-renewals cap, state-agency approval (PED beyond notice), or annual-termination clause might exist somewhere in § 22-16-3, 6.43.2 NMAC, or 6.41.4 NMAC.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read § 22-16-3 in full from the NM PED PDF and HB 485 PDF; re-read 6.43.2.8, 6.43.2.12, 6.43.2.13, and 6.43.2.14 NMAC. Searched for 'escalator', 'CPI', 'index', 'evergreen', 'automatic', 'termination notice', 'public hearing', 'maximum renewals'.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding holds. The renewal mechanism is exactly as Stage 1 stated: at the local board's option, annually, on the same terms and conditions, with the 6.43.2.12 NMAC procedural prerequisites (financial statement, ownership disclosure, evaluation, notice to PED). The PED-approved contract forms include for-cause termination but no annual-termination right; this differs from a 'multi-year with annual termination permitted' framing.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confidence remains High on renewal mechanics.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-education transportation follows the same 5-year-plus-annual-renewal rule
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Separate state-law contracting/procurement rules might exist in 6.31.2 NMAC (special education), 6.41.4 NMAC (transportation operations), 22-16 series statutes, or federal IDEA implementing regulations for IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, alternative, or homeless-student transportation procured from private contractors.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read 6.41.4 NMAC in full (PED PDF); searched 6.31.2 NMAC for transportation-contracting provisions; re-fetched NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB. Verified that 34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.107 (IDEA) and McKinney-Vento address the duty to provide transportation, not state contract terms.
challengeMatrix.result: Finding holds. 6.41.4 NMAC contains no contract-term-limit provisions; 6.31.2 NMAC contains no transportation-contracting rules; NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB references 6.41.4 NMAC and federal IDEA only - no separate state contracting rule. The § 22-16-3 / 6.43.2 NMAC framework applies uniformly to all school bus service contracts regardless of student population.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confidence remains High on special-education differentiation.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District Variance - 'Unknown' (no authoritative survey of district-level practice located)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Differing district practice might be documented in an AG opinion, State Auditor report, PED bulletin, or in widely divergent district policy manuals - or alternatively, uniform statewide application might be conclusively established by official guidance.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched nmdoj.gov AG opinions; searched State Auditor reports; spot-checked Albuquerque Public Schools and Las Cruces Public Schools procurement / transportation pages; reviewed NMSBA sample policies; searched for variance language across PED bulletins.
challengeMatrix.result: No credible disconfirming authority located - and no credible authority establishing definitive statewide uniformity beyond the uniform-PED-form requirement. APS and LCPS appear to operate within the § 22-16-3 framework, but two-district spot-checks do not establish statewide uniformity. The negative-result finding stands.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confidence stays at Unknown / unresolved (a sourced negative result that itself survives the challenge round).

sourcesReverified.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 (School Bus Service Contracts), NM Public Education Department's official PDF posting
sourcesReverified.note: Full text of subsections A-E extracted via PDF-to-text and matched verbatim to Stage 1's quotes; amendment history confirmed (1953 Comp § 77-14-3; Laws 1967 ch. 16 § 221; 1993 ch. 226 § 47; 1995 ch. 208 § 6; 2009 ch. 92 § 2).

sourcesReverified.citation: Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485, 2009 Regular Session) - last amendment to § 22-16-3
sourcesReverified.note: Session-law PDF retrieved from nmlegis.gov; OCR-extracted full text confirms Stage 1's verbatim quotes; applicability section (Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 3) confirmed: 'This act applies to contracts, including contract renewals, entered into on or after June 19, 2009.'

sourcesReverified.citation: NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98 (Exemptions from the Procurement Code), Subsection H
sourcesReverified.note: 2012 NM Procurement Code compendium published on the NM Supreme Court website confirms verbatim: 'H. contracts with businesses for public school transportation services'. Morningstar annotation confirms the Code's exemptions apply to school districts. Stage 1's cited Justia URL returned HTTP 403 on re-verification and was replaced with this official source.

2012 compendium confirms the structure; current law for the $25k+ tier is 10 years (raised from 8 by a later amendment as confirmed by Cuddy & McCarthy presentation and Justia 2025 compilation snippet). Section is contextual only - does not control because of § 13-1-98(H) exemption. Stage 1's Justia URL returned HTTP 403 on re-verification.

sourcesReverified.citation: 6.43.2 NMAC (Requirements for School Bus Contracts and Per Capita Feeder Agreements) - Sections 6.43.2.8, 6.43.2.11, 6.43.2.12, 6.43.2.13, 6.43.2.14
sourcesReverified.note: NM State Records Center and Archives (official NMAC publisher) re-confirmed verbatim the five-year-term language in 6.43.2.8(G), the RFP-only-for-new-contracts procedure in 6.43.2.11(B), the renewal procedure in 6.43.2.12, and the 'renewed annually ... at the option of the BOARD' language in the TERM clauses of the PED-approved fleet contract form (6.43.2.13(A)) and individual owner-operator contract form (6.43.2.14(A)). Confirmed: no annual-termination clause in the forms (only for-cause termination).

sourcesReverified.citation: 6.41.4 NMAC (Standards for Providing Transportation for Eligible Students)
sourcesReverified.note: PED current rule PDF retrieved; PDF-to-text extraction confirms initial effective date of 12/31/1998, with subsequent amendments through 11/15/2005 and 9/29/2017 (section-level). Rule contains no contract-term-limit or competitive-procurement provisions for special-education or any other student-population transportation contracts.

sourcesReverified.citation: NMSBA Sample Policy EE (Transportation Services), adopted July 9, 2018
sourcesReverified.note: Confirms 'school bus service contracts for up to five (5) years approved by the School Board on forms approved by the Public Education Department (PED)'. Legal references match Stage 1: NMSA 22-8-27, 22-8-29, 22-16-1 et seq.; 6.41.4 NMAC.

sourcesReverified.citation: NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB (Special Education Student Transportation), adopted July 9, 2018
sourcesReverified.note: Confirms policy contains 'no provisions about contract term limits or procurement procedures' for special-education transportation; legal references are limited to 6.41.4 NMAC and 34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.104, 300.107.

sourcesReverified.citation: Cuddy & McCarthy, LLP - 'Procurement Code: What Districts Need to Know' (NMSBA-hosted presentation)
sourcesReverified.note: PDF retrieved and OCR-extracted; confirms verbatim: 'Contracts for public school transportation services' listed among items 'Exempt from the Procurement Code' under NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98. Confirms § 13-1-150 contract-length tiers ($25k+ not to exceed 10 years; professional services not to exceed 4 years).

sourcesReverified.citation: Justia 2025 N.M. Stat. - NMSA § 13-1-98 (Stage 1's original URL)
sourcesReverified.note: Justia URL returned HTTP 403 Forbidden on WebFetch re-verification. Substantive verification continued against the NM Supreme Court 2012 Procurement Code compendium (official tier-1 source); citation in the ledger now points to the Supreme Court PDF as the primary URL.

sourcesReverified.citation: Justia 2025 N.M. Stat. - NMSA § 13-1-150 (Stage 1's original URL)

Substantive verification continued against the NM Supreme Court 2012 Procurement Code compendium (official tier-1 source) and the Cuddy & McCarthy presentation; citation in the ledger now points to the Supreme Court PDF as the primary URL.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Codified state statute (official PED PDF)
sourceLedger.citation: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 (School Bus Service Contracts), as posted by the NM Public Education Department on its School Transportation State Statutes and Regulations page.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: PED PDF dated August 26, 2024 by author 'Alyssa Marquez' per PDF metadata; statutory text matches Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2; no later amendments located in 2010-2026 session searches.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'D. A school district may enter into a school bus service contract for a term not to exceed five years. A school bus service contract may provide, at the expiration of the term of the contract, for annual renewal of the school bus service contract on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local school board.' History: 1953 Comp., § 77-14-3, enacted by Laws 1967, ch. 16, § 221; 1993, ch. 226, § 47; 1995, ch. 208, § 6; 2009, ch. 92, § 2.

sourceLedger.citation: Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485, 2009 Regular Session) - last amendment to NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 (and conforming amendments to § 22-8-27). Applicability (§ 3): 'This act applies to contracts, including contract renewals, entered into on or after June 19, 2009.'
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective June 19, 2009; PDF metadata shows committee substitute final version dated April 7, 2009. No later session-law amendment to § 22-16-3 located.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation; Renewal Mechanics; Max Contract Term
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section 2: 'A school district may enter into a school bus service contract for a term not to exceed five years. A school bus service contract may provide, at the expiration of the term of the contract, for annual renewal of the school bus service contract on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local school board.' Section 3 (APPLICABILITY): 'This act applies to contracts, including contract renewals, entered into on or after June 19, 2009.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Codified state statute (NM Supreme Court official compendium)
sourceLedger.citation: NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98 (Exemptions from the Procurement Code), Subsection H.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2012 compendium published on the NM Supreme Court website; § 13-1-98(H) text remains unchanged through subsequent amendments per Justia 2025 compilation and Cuddy & McCarthy 2018 presentation. The 2009 amendment (effective July 1, 2009) added Subsection EE; the H exemption for public school transportation predates that amendment.
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The provisions of the Procurement Code shall not apply to: ... H. contracts with businesses for public school transportation services'. Annotation (Morningstar Water Users Ass'n v. Farmington Mun. Sch. Dist. No. 5, 1995-NMSC-052): § 13-1-98's exemptions apply to school district contracts via § 22-5-4(N)'s incorporation of the Procurement Code.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 - Codified state statute (NM Supreme Court official compendium) - contextual only
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2012 compendium prints the $25k+ tier at 8 years; current law (per Cuddy & McCarthy 2018 presentation and Justia 2025 compilation) is 10 years for the $25k+ tier and 4 years for professional services. Section was amended in 2025 (Laws 2025, ch. 86, § 14) to add a Trade Ports Development Act 30-year carve-out. The section remains contextual only; does not control school bus contracts because of § 13-1-98(H) exemption.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (context only - explains general procurement multi-term rule that does NOT apply to school bus contracts)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Subsection A: tangible personal property, construction or services (except professional services) - under $25,000, term not to exceed 4 years; $25,000 or more, term not to exceed [10] years (including all extensions and renewals), with Public Facility Energy Efficiency and Water Conservation Act exception (25 years) and 2025 Trade Ports Development Act exception (30 years). Subsection B: professional services not to exceed 4 years (with five enumerated exceptions).

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - State administrative code (NM Records Center / Archives - official NMAC publisher)
sourceLedger.citation: 6.43.2 NMAC (Requirements for School Bus Contracts and Per Capita Feeder Agreements). Statutory authority (6.43.2.3 NMAC): NMSA 1978, §§ 22-2-1, 22-16-2, 22-16-3, 22-16-6, and 22-8-27. Sections referenced: 6.43.2.8 (school bus service contracts - five-year term with annual renewal at board option); 6.43.2.11 (procedures for awarding NEW contracts - RFP, ten-day notice, board-developed evaluation criteria); 6.43.2.12 (procedures for renewing existing contracts - financial statement, ownership disclosure, evaluation, notice to PED; no rebid); 6.43.2.13 (fleet service contract form - PED-approved); 6.43.2.14 (individual owner-operator equipment contract form - PED-approved).
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective 12-31-1998; last amended 11-13-2009 per the official text published by the NM State Records Center and Archives.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; District Variance (uniform PED-approved contract forms)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 6.43.2.8(G): 'The local board may agree to a five-year contract period. Any changes in the terms of the contract require that the contract be renegotiated/amended. At the end of the contract period the contract may be renewed annually on the same terms and conditions at the option of the local board.' 6.43.2.11(B): 'The local board shall publish notice of a request for proposal (RFP) for procurement of transportation services not less than ten calendar days prior to the date set for the receipt of the proposals.' 6.43.2.12 (renewal procedures): 'A. A current financial statement. B. If a corporation, the names, addresses, dates of birth, and social security numbers of all stockholders or partnerships. C. The local board shall evaluate the services that have been rendered by the contractor on the basis of the terms of the contract. D. ... If the contract is recommended for renewal, the terms of the contract shall ensure that any deficiencies identified in the evaluation are corrected. ... G. The local board shall provide notice of the contract renewal to the state transportation director on forms provided by the public education department.' 6.43.2.13(A) and 6.43.2.14(A) (PED-approved contract forms): 'The term of this agreement shall commence ___, 20___ and shall continue through ___, 20___. This contract may be renewed annually thereafter on the same terms and conditions at the option of the BOARD.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 - State administrative code (NM Public Education Department - operations rule)
sourceLedger.citation: 6.41.4 NMAC - Standards for Providing Transportation for Eligible Students. Statutory authority: NMSA 1978, §§ 22-2-1, 22-2-2, 22-8-26, 22-10A-5, 22-16-2.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Initial effective date 12-31-1998; subsequent amendments at section level through 11-15-2005 and 9-29-2017. Current rule posted by NM PED in January 2025.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation (confirms operational rules only; no separate term/procurement cap)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Regulates route requirements, driver qualifications, bus operations, route review cycles, instructional programs for school bus drivers, and SUV use; covers regular routes, special-education transportation, IEP students, foster youth, and homeless youth. Does NOT impose any separate contract-term or competitive-procurement requirement for special-education transportation contracts. Requires 'properly executed contract on a form approved by the department' but specifies no duration restrictions.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 5 - School-boards-association sample policy citing binding statute/rule
sourceLedger.citation: New Mexico School Boards Association - Sample Policy EE (Transportation Services). Legal references: NMSA 1978, §§ 22-8-27, 22-8-29, 22-16-1 et seq.; 6.41.4 NMAC et seq. Adopted July 9, 2018.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Sample policy adopted July 9, 2018; cites the 22-16 series and 6.41.4 NMAC; consistent with current law as of 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Transportation services for students may be provided through use of school bus service contracts for up to five (5) years approved by the School Board on forms approved by the Public Education Department (PED). The District shall follow the rules of PED pertaining to the parameters of such contracts including placing a lien on each contractor school bus.'

sourceLedger.citation: NMSBA Sample Policy EEAB - Special Education Student Transportation. Legal references: 6.41.4.8, 6.41.4.9, 6.41.4.11, 6.41.4.13, 6.41.4.14 NMAC; 34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.104, 300.107. Adopted July 9, 2018.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Adopted July 9, 2018; cross-references current administrative code and federal IDEA regulations.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Cites only 6.41.4 NMAC sections (general transportation operations) and federal IDEA regulations (34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.104, 300.107) for special-education transportation; contains no provisions about contract term limits or procurement procedures, confirming reliance on the standard NMSA 22-16-3 / 6.43.2 NMAC framework.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 5 - Law-firm presentation to NMSBA-published practitioner audience, citing binding statutes
sourceLedger.citation: Cuddy & McCarthy, LLP - 'Procurement Code: What Districts Need to Know' (presentation hosted on NMSBA website). Cites NMSA 1978, §§ 13-1-29, 13-1-30, 13-1-98, 13-1-102, 13-1-150, 22-5-4, 22-21-1.
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Practitioner-facing slide deck (2018); confirmed statutory positions matching current statutes accessed 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Lists 'Contracts for public school transportation services' among items 'Exempt from the Procurement Code' under NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98. On contract length under § 13-1-150 (which does not apply to bus contracts because of the H exemption): 'For tangible personal property, construction or services, except professional services, and when the contract amount is less than $25k, any length of time deemed in the District's best interest not to exceed 4 years. Contracts exceeding $25k, not to exceed 10 years. Professional services, not to exceed 4 years.'

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Adversarial re-verification began by re-opening every official URL cited in Stage 1. Where a Justia URL returned HTTP 403 on WebFetch (the cited § 13-1-98 and § 13-1-150 Justia pages), the cited text was re-verified against an official tier-1 source - the NM Supreme Court's 2012 NM Procurement Code compendium - and the Justia URL was replaced with the official Supreme Court PDF in the source ledger. The NM PED's official posting of NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 (a higher-tier source than the session-law PDF cited by Stage 1) was located and adopted as the primary citation. Every claim was challenged using the disconfirming-pattern checklist (longer-term/grandfather/notwithstanding searches for max term; sole-source/cooperative-purchasing/mandatory-bidding searches for rebid; escalator/evergreen/automatic-renewal/annual-termination searches for renewal mechanics; separate IDEA/McKinney-Vento/foster-care/homeless-student procurement rule searches for special-education; AG opinion / State Auditor / large-district + small-district policy spot-checks for district variance). Where source text was returned as binary PDF via WebFetch, the saved PDF was extracted to plain text using pdftotext.
queriesThatMattered: 'NMSA 1978' '13-1-98' 'public school transportation services' exemption procurement code
'NMSA' '22-16-3' 'school bus service contract' 'five years' annual renewal
'22-16-3' New Mexico amended 2023 2024 2025 school bus contract legislation
'13-1-150' New Mexico procurement 'multi-term' 2025 'not to exceed' years current
New Mexico special education transportation contract procurement statute different term IEP
New Mexico Attorney General opinion '22-16-3' OR 'school bus' contract term
'Albuquerque Public Schools' transportation contract bus services RFP term renewal policy
'Las Cruces Public Schools' OR 'Rio Rancho Public Schools' school bus contract term renewal policy
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: NMSA 1978, § 22-16-3 - current through Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (effective June 19, 2009); no later amendments located in targeted 2010-2026 session searches via nmlegis.gov, NM Bill Tracker, and the NMPED Bill Analyses index. NMSA 1978, § 13-1-98 - current through Laws 2009, ch. 231 (effective July 1, 2009) per the NM Supreme Court 2012 compendium; subsequent amendments to other subsections do not affect Subsection H. NMSA 1978, § 13-1-150 - current through Laws 2025, ch. 86, § 14 (effective July 1, 2025) per Justia 2025 compilation; remains contextual only.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: 6.43.2 NMAC - effective 12-31-1998, last amended 11-13-2009 per the NM State Records Center and Archives posting. 6.41.4 NMAC - effective 12-31-1998 base; section-level amendments through 9-29-2017 per the NM PED current-rule PDF posted January 2025.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Re-verified Laws 2009, ch. 92, § 2 (HB 485) as the last amendment to § 22-16-3. Searched nmlegis.gov 2010-2026 session bills for amendments to § 22-16-3 or the 22-16 series - none located. NMPED Bill Analyses index and NM Bill Tracker checked for 2024-2026 session bills affecting school bus contracts - none located. 2024 HB 75 (electric/zero-emission school bus replacement) does NOT amend § 22-16-3 or the contract-term framework. February 2026 PED update referenced in the NM PED page relates to parental reimbursement statute, not § 22-16-3.
negativeSearchPaths: Searched for a NM Attorney General opinion interpreting § 22-16-3 contract-term or renewal mechanics - none located on the NM DOJ publications page or via general web search.
Searched for a State Auditor report flagging district-level interpretive variance under § 22-16-3 - none located.

Searched for differing district policy manuals interpreting § 22-16-3 (variance question) - NMSBA sample policies EE and EEAB cite the statute uniformly; spot-checks of Albuquerque Public Schools and Las Cruces Public Schools materials show both districts operating within the standard § 22-16-3 framework; no contrary district-policy interpretation located.

Searched for separate state-law term caps or procurement rules for special-education / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care transportation contracts - none located in 6.31.2 NMAC, 6.41.4 NMAC, or 6.43.2 NMAC. Federal IDEA (34 C.F.R. §§ 300.34, 300.107) and McKinney-Vento govern the duty to provide transportation, not state contract terms.

Searched for CPI/index escalator, fuel escalator, evergreen/automatic-rollover provisions, annual-termination clause, public-hearing requirement, maximum-number-of-renewals cap, or third-party-agency approval in § 22-16-3 or 6.43.2 NMAC - none located beyond the requirement that contract amounts reflect fuel and operation/maintenance costs (§ 22-16-3(C); 6.43.2.8(D) NMAC).
Searched for any 'notwithstanding'/'successive'/'extension'/'grandfather' provision in § 22-16-3 - none located.
Searched for any carve-back to § 13-1-98(H)'s categorical exemption that would re-impose Procurement Code competitive-bidding requirements on school bus contracts - none located.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: Reconciled an apparent conflict between Stage 1's stated $25k+ tier of § 13-1-150 (10 years) and the 2012 NM Supreme Court compendium (which prints '8 years' as that compendium predates a later amendment raising the cap to 10 years). Current law (Cuddy & McCarthy 2018 presentation; Justia 2025 compilation) is 10 years. The discrepancy is immaterial because § 13-1-98(H) categorically removes school bus contracts from § 13-1-150's reach. Reconciled an apparent discrepancy in the WebFetch result on 6.41.4 NMAC (which reported an 'initial effective date: March 27, 2018') against the actual PED current-rule PDF (which shows base effective date 12-31-1998 with section-level amendments through 9-29-2017). The PDF text is authoritative; Stage 1's effective-date statement is correct.

unresolvedFields: District-Level Interpretation Variance - recorded as 'Unknown' because no authoritative survey, AG opinion, State Auditor report, or PED bulletin documenting variant district interpretations was located. The uniformity of PED-approved contract forms (6.43.2.13 and 6.43.2.14 NMAC) and statewide NMSBA sample policies suggests uniform application, but the variance question cannot be answered definitively from official sources. Adversarial challenge attempted via large + small district spot-checks (Albuquerque Public Schools, Las Cruces Public Schools) found both districts operating within the § 22-16-3 framework, but two-district spot-checks do not establish statewide uniformity.
